html:
Exclusive to:
Only during the Event: The Gorge

GenericStumpTapped emptyTapped readyTapped bugsWounded

Sugarwood Trees are Plants exclusive to The Gorge event in Don't Starve Together. They can only be found alongside Spotty Shrubs in the Sugarwood Memorial Park area that can only be accessed after buying an Iron Key from Sammy.

Tapping them with Tree Tapping Kits allows the players to obtain Sap which is required to cook Syrup. Each tapped Tree grants one Sap which can be gathered either by harvesting the Tapping Kit or removing it completely from the Tree with an Axe. If too much time passes before the Sap is harvested from a tapped Tree, it will become Ruined Sap which cannot be used for cooking.

Sugarwood Trees can also be chopped with an Axe to acquire Logs and Twigs, the amount obtained depending on the size of the Tree. Digging up the remaining stump with a Shovel grants an additional Log.

Trivia

  • The Extra Sappy Accomplishment is given for gathering Sap from tapped Sugarwood Trees 9 times or more in a single The Gorge match.
  • The appearance of this tree and the way of harvesting their sap to cook into Syrup is based on Maple trees, which themselves are tapped to harvest and cook maple syrup.

Gallery

  • The Sugarwood Memorial Park area containing Sugarwood Trees and Spotty Shrubs.
  • Some tapped Sugarwood Trees.
  • An infested Sugarwood Tree producing Ruined Sap.
  • A Sugarwood Tree that has been tapped with a Tree Tapping Kit.
  • A tapped Sugarwood Tree that is ready to be harvested.
  • A tapped Sugarwood Tree that has become infested.
  • An infested Sugarwood Tree.
  • A Sugarwood Tree stump.
  • Concept art
  • Extra Sappy Accomplishment.

GenericStumpTapped emptyTapped readyTapped bugsWounded
“It's full of delicious, delicious sap.”
–Wilson
“Aw! It's impervious to fire.”
–Willow
“Is little tree of yummy goop.”
–Wolfgang
“It has a sickly beauty.”
–Wendy
“A HIDEOUS PINK TREE”
–WX-78
“What unique coloring. I should like to press its leaves in my books!”
–Wickerbottom
“It's beautiful!”
–Woodie
“A sickly sweet aroma wafts from its branches.”
–Maxwell
“The nectar öf the göds dwells within.”
–Wigfrid
“I wonder why the people left this nice place.”
–Webber
“Sweet Friend!”
–Wormwood
“It's a big, pink tree.”
–Winona
“Fweehee, what a special tree!”
–Wortox
“There sweet stuff inside!”
–Wurt
“It's full of delicious, delicious sap.”
–Walter
“It's full of delicious, delicious sap.”
–Wanda
“Where'd the tree go? I'm stumped.”
–Wilson
“It's kinda useless now.”
–Willow
“Tree has been cut down!”
–Wolfgang
“All things must end.”
–Wendy
“THE SOURCE OF PLANT COOLANT HAS BEEN DESTROYED”
–WX-78
“I should at least like to study its rings.”
–Wickerbottom
“Cut down in its prime.”
–Woodie
“It won't produce sap now.”
–Maxwell
“It has been chöpped.”
–Wigfrid
“Why would anyone do that?”
–Webber
“Sorry”
–Wormwood
“That ain't growin' back.”
–Winona
“That's a wrap on the sap.”
–Wortox
“Someone took tree! Not me!”
–Wurt
“Where'd the tree go? I'm stumped.”
–Walter
“Where'd the tree go? I'm stumped.”
–Wanda
“Here sappy, sappy, sap.”
–Wilson
“It's taking forever!”
–Willow
“Little bucket needs to fill up.”
–Wolfgang
“Like a dagger through the heart. A tree heart.”
–Wendy
“I DEMAND YOU FILL UP FASTER”
–WX-78
“Sap collection is under way.”
–Wickerbottom
“Fillin' up, slow as molasses.”
–Woodie
“It is filling. Ever so slowly.”
–Maxwell
“I've nö patience för thee, sap!”
–Wigfrid
“There isn't any sap yet.”
–Webber
“Oh, friend. Sorry”
–Wormwood
“I wish trees leaked a little faster.”
–Winona
“The tap will soon make sap!”
–Wortox
“Aww... nothing left.”
–Wurt
“Here sappy, sappy, sap.”
–Walter
“Here sappy, sappy, sap.”
–Wanda
“Sweet golden sap.”
–Wilson
“Sap's ready!”
–Willow
“Little bucket is full!”
–Wolfgang
“I can harvest it now.”
–Wendy
“IT IS TIME TO REMOVE THE BUCKET”
–WX-78
“No sense letting it sit.”
–Wickerbottom
“Thanks for the sap, tree.”
–Woodie
“The sap is ready for collection.”
–Maxwell
“I have acquired the nectar!”
–Wigfrid
“Sticky, sticky sap!”
–Webber
“Full of friend insides”
–Wormwood
“That bucket's practically overflowing!”
–Winona
“Sweet, sugary sap!”
–Wortox
“Mmm, it full of sweet stuff!”
–Wurt
“Sweet golden sap.”
–Walter
“Sweet golden sap.”
–Wanda
“That's how you get ants.”
–Wilson
“Stupid bugs.”
–Willow
“Wolfgang will crush tiny creatures.”
–Wolfgang
“All that sacrifice for nothing.”
–Wendy
“TINY BIOLOGICAL UNITS HAVE FOILED COOLANT COLLECTION”
–WX-78
“The insecta are drawn to the glucose.”
–Wickerbottom
“Hey, get away from that tree will ya!”
–Woodie
“Well it's ruined now.”
–Maxwell
“It hath been föuled by tiny beasts!”
–Wigfrid
“Ew... The bugs got in it.”
–Webber
“Bugs hurt friend!”
–Wormwood
“It's a little buggy.”
–Winona
“Those bugs are tree thugs!”
–Wortox
“Blegh, there bugs stuck in the sweet stuff.”
–Wurt
“That's how you get ants.”
–Walter
“That's how you get ants.”
–Wanda
“It looks ill.”
–Wilson
“You don't look so hot, tree.”
–Willow
“Little tree has boo-boo.”
–Wolfgang
“Its life ebbs.”
–Wendy
“TRUNK HAS SUSTAINED DAMAGE”
–WX-78
“Its internal structure has been damaged, but it can still recover.”
–Wickerbottom
“What troubles you, sister?”
–Woodie
“This tree has seen better days.”
–Maxwell
“It hath been wöunded!”
–Wigfrid
“Aw, now it's not as pink.”
–Webber
“OUCH! Friend hurt”
–Wormwood
“It's lost some of its color.”
–Winona
“An unfortunate mishap befell the tree sap.”
–Wortox
“Tree okay?”
–Wurt
“It looks ill.”
–Walter
“It looks ill.”
–Wanda
Sugarwood Trees are Plants exclusive to The Gorge event in Don't Starve Together. They can only be found alongside Spotty Shrubs in the Sugarwood Memorial Park area that can only be accessed after buying an Iron Key from Sammy.
Tapping them with Tree Tapping Kits allows the players to obtain Sap which is required to cook Syrup. If too much time passes before the Sap is harvested from a tapped Tree, it will become Ruined Sap which cannot be used for cooking.
Sugarwood Trees can also be chopped with an Axe to acquire Logs and Twigs, the amount obtained depending on the size of the Tree. Digging up the remaining stump with a Shovel grants an additional Log.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Cây Sugarwood là Thực vật độc quyền cho sự kiện The Gorge trong Don't Starve Together. Chúng chỉ có thể được tìm thấy cùng với Spotty Shrub trong khu vực Công viên tưởng niệm Sugarwood và chỉ có thể truy cập được sau khi mua Chìa khóa sắt từ Sammy.
